app.name: CloudSaw
empty.no_scans.title: No scans yet
empty.no_scans.body: Connect an AWS account from the Accounts tab to run your first scan.
home.heading: Welcome to CloudSaw
home.body: Scan AWS accounts for misconfigurations — fully local, no data leaves your machine.
applock.disclosure: The app lock prevents casual access only. It does not encrypt data on disk — use full-disk encryption (FileVault, BitLocker, LUKS) to protect against device theft.
applock.setup.title: Set a master password
applock.setup.subtitle: Before you can use CloudSaw, choose a password that will lock the app window.
applock.unlock.title: CloudSaw is locked
applock.unlock.subtitle: Enter your master password to continue.
applock.recovery.title: Reset your password
applock.recovery.reason: Verify your identity to reset CloudSaw's master password
applock.recovery.explainer: Your operating system will ask you to confirm your identity (device PIN, password, or passkey). On success you can choose a new master password — the old one is not revealed.
applock.recovery.unavailable_hint: Recovery isn't available on this device. You must remember your password or reinstall CloudSaw.
applock.settings.subtitle: Manage how CloudSaw locks and re-prompts for your password.
applock.settings.period.label: Re-lock after
applock.settings.period.never_warning: CloudSaw won't re-prompt for the password. Use "Lock now" or set a re-lock period to restore the gate.
applock.settings.biometric.label: Allow biometric unlock
applock.settings.biometric.description: Use Windows Hello, Touch ID, or your device's user-presence prompt to unlock CloudSaw. Password unlock still works as a fallback.
applock.settings.biometric.unavailable: This device doesn't expose a biometric prompt to CloudSaw.
applock.settings.biometric.unconfigured: Biometric hardware is present but not currently configured in the OS.
applock.error.rate_limited: Too many attempts. Try again in {seconds}s.
applock.error.already_configured: A master password is already set.
applock.error.identity_unavailable: Identity verification isn't available on this device.
applock.error.generic: Something went wrong with the app lock.
profiles.title: AWS profiles
profiles.subtitle: Profiles discovered from ~/.aws/config. CloudSaw never reads ~/.aws/credentials directly — credentials are resolved by the AWS SDK at scan time.
profiles.empty.title: No AWS profiles found
profiles.empty.body: Run `aws configure` or `aws configure sso` from a terminal, then click "Refresh profiles".
accounts.title: Accounts
accounts.subtitle: AWS accounts you've configured CloudSaw to scan. The active account is the target for new scans and reports.
accounts.section.configured_hint: Each account ties a label to one AWS profile. The AWS account ID is verified against sts:GetCallerIdentity before the account is saved.
accounts.reveal_toggle.label: Show full account IDs
accounts.empty.title: No accounts configured
accounts.empty.body: Add an account to point CloudSaw at an AWS profile. CloudSaw verifies the profile and stores the resulting AWS account ID — never credentials.
accounts.row.profile_missing_hint: Profile "{profile}" is no longer in ~/.aws/config. Re-add it with `aws configure` (or update this account to point at a different profile).
accounts.add.title: Add an AWS account
accounts.add.explainer: CloudSaw will call sts:GetCallerIdentity on the selected profile to verify the account ID. No credentials are stored.
accounts.add.label_hint: How this account appears in CloudSaw (e.g. "acme-dev"). Must be unique.
accounts.add.profile_hint: The profile CloudSaw resolves credentials through. Pick one from ~/.aws/config.
accounts.add.no_profiles: No profiles found in ~/.aws/config. Run `aws configure` or `aws configure sso` and reopen this dialog.
accounts.edit.title: Edit account
accounts.edit.explainer: Changing the profile re-verifies the AWS account ID. If the new profile resolves to a different account, the change is rejected — remove this account and add a new one instead.
accounts.edit.profile_hint: Change the profile only if you've reconfigured ~/.aws/config to use a different name for the same account.
accounts.remove.title: Remove account
accounts.remove.explainer: This will permanently remove the CloudSaw record for "{label}". The data below will be deleted from your machine. The AWS account itself is not affected.
accounts.remove.permanence: Removal is immediate and permanent — there's no undo.
accounts.error.not_found: That account is no longer in CloudSaw.
accounts.error.duplicate_aws_account_id: Another CloudSaw account already uses this AWS account ID.
accounts.error.duplicate_label: That label is already taken by another account.
accounts.error.aws_account_id_mismatch: The chosen profile resolves to a different AWS account. Remove this account and add the new profile instead.
aws.error.config_unreadable: Couldn't read your AWS config file.
aws.error.timeout: AWS didn't respond in time. Check your network and try again.
aws.error.connectivity: Couldn't reach AWS. Check your network.
aws.error.sso_expired: Your SSO session is expired. Run `aws sso login` and try again.
aws.error.permission_denied: AWS denied the request. The profile lacks permission for this API.
aws.failure.sso_expired: SSO session is expired. Run `aws sso login`.
